name: Architext 1.6.1 Repository Intelligence and Diagram Config
summary: Architext 1.6.1 carries the repository-intelligence and architecture-annotation work originally slated for 1.6.0, adds user-configurable diagram parameters (persisted in ~/.architext config), folds in the App-component decomposition and crowded-face routing refinements deferred from 1.6.0, and includes minor UI cleanup.
updateSource: 1.6.1 shipped: GitHub release v1.6.1 published; publish.yml (OIDC) succeeded incl. packed-CLI smoke test; npm dist-tag latest=1.6.1.

planned.title: Repository tree view
planned.summary: Add a top-level Repo Tree tab that helps users understand source layout, optionally color-coding folders tied to C4 or Flow responsibilities and leaving room for a future 3D graph projection. DELIVERED: a Repo Tree top-bar mode listing the repository's tracked files (git ls-files, filesystem fallback excluding node_modules/dist/.git) as a folder tree, each path colored by the architecture node that owns it via node.sourcePaths (longest-prefix match). C4-type and Flow color lenses toggle the palette; folders show a dominant/mixed owner hint; clicking an owned file selects its component in the detail panel.
evidence: Live corpus-fixture review: 33 files attributed, both lenses, file->node detail panel, 0 console errors

planned.title: Repository blast-radius search
planned.summary: Provide search for filenames, components, or known code concepts so users can see where a concern reaches across repository files and architecture elements. DELIVERED: a Blast Radius top-bar mode. Searching ranks component matches (name/id/sourcePath/summary/responsibilities) and file matches (resolved to their owning component). Focusing a component shows its full reach: owned files, forward dependencies, reverse dependents (nodes whose dependencies include it), participating flows (related + step endpoints), decisions, risks, data handled, and views. Component chips re-focus the panel; flow/view chips navigate out; the detail panel stays in sync.
evidence: Live corpus review: search -> focus (Auth reaches 14 elements) -> chip re-focus (forward + reverse deps) -> flow navigation, 0 console errors

planned.title: Diagram spacing controls
planned.summary: Add explicit ergonomics controls for diagram column width and sequence-diagram row spacing so users can improve readability without relying only on zoom. DELIVERED by the diagram configuration panel (column width + row/sequence spacing live-preview controls, Save to project/user). Folded into diagram-parameter-user-config.

planned.title: Persistent architecture element notes
planned.summary: Allow users to edit or annotate architecture elements and persist the notes in Architext JSON, including cases where a high-risk area is intentionally mitigated by the system being documented. DELIVERED: notes are a new optional data file (notes.json + manifest.files.notes, additive to the manifest schema) keyed by target {kind, id} with a category (note/mitigation/caveat/todo), body, and timestamps. The viewer detail panel gains a Notes section with add/edit/delete; writes POST /api/notes, which self-bootstraps manifest.files.notes + notes.json on first use and applies the write under the target lock with write->validate->rollback. The validator + viewer reference checks enforce that a note's target id exists. DEFERRED: the editor is exposed on node and flow detail panels (the selectable elements); risk/decision/view targeting is model- and validation-ready but not yet exposed in the UI because those elements are not independently selectable in the viewer yet (exposing them is a separate selection-surface change), so risk annotation is currently recorded on the owning component.
evidence: viewer NotesSection.tsx + DetailPanel (node + flow); fetchArchitectureData loads notes

Live corpus review: add+delete on a flow persisted, validated, reloaded, rendered; node detail renders the same editor; 0 console errors. Also fixed /data/* Cache-Control: no-store so post-write reloads are not stale.

planned.title: User-configurable diagram parameters
planned.summary: Move hardcoded diagram parameters (e.g. minimum legible gap as an arrowhead fraction, spacing, column width) into user-adjustable configuration persisted under ~/.architext, so projects can tune layout without code changes. DELIVERED: layout (column width, row gap, node size, route gutter, top margin), sequence-diagram spacing, and fit-zoom bounds are user-configurable via ~/.architext/config.json + docs/architext/config.json and a live-preview settings panel (Save to project/user). DEFERRED: the legibility-gap arrowhead-fraction knob is split to routing-polish because applying it requires threading MIN_LEGIBLE_GAP_ARROWHEADS through the router (routing-engine work), not just view config; the config field is accepted/persisted but not yet applied.

planned.title: Fix decision-diamond branch routing
planned.summary: The viewer renders decision-diamond branches poorly, exposed by the first decision example in the corpus (feature-showcase). Evidence (rendered geometry): both outcome branches share one segment out of the diamond (485,199)->(772,199) instead of fanning, and each overshoots its target node by ~36px and doubles back (approved ->(772,145)->(736,145); rejected ->(772,224)->(736,224)) — a dogleg/stub of the class 1.6.0 eliminated for normal edges. Decision-branch routing was never exercised before because no diagram had a decision. Fan the branches from the diamond and remove the overshoot. FIXED (commit 3ddc153): root cause was preferredDecisionBranchEndSide returning the same side as the exit for forward-column branches, so the router mounted the targets far face and wrapped back. Now mounts the facing side; branches fan from distinct tips; node-facing point reserved (supports up to 3 outcomes); diamond reconnected to its node with a node-colour-matched connector. Verified in the live UI via DOM geometry (0 overshoot, 0 staircase) + unit tests.

planned.title: Minor UI cleanup
planned.summary: Assorted minor viewer UI cleanup items collected during the routing overhaul review. DELIVERED by absorption: the routing-review nits were addressed across the 1.6.1 polish passes — steps-toggle width stabilization (#90), responsive top-bar tab wrapping (#93), the repo-tree/blast-radius/notes design-critique remediations (#89/#91/#92), and the design-language consolidation (#94/#96). No concrete items remain in the bucket.

planned.title: Decompose the App god-component
planned.summary: Extract App (viewer/src/main.tsx) into useArchitextModel / useDiagramViewport / useNavigation hooks. Carried into 1.6.1 from 1.6.0. It was not taken inside the 1.6.0 cut because it is a structural refactor with NO behavior change (not a correctness or release blocker), the viewer source relocated since the audit plan was written (docs/architext/src -> viewer/src) so it needs re-scoping against the new layout, and there is no automated UI harness for App so it carries manual-verification risk best taken outside a release cut. RESOLVED: two of the three planned hooks already exist as extracted modules (viewer/src/presentation/useArchitextModel.ts, useDiagramViewport.ts) plus useUnsavedEditorGuard. The remaining useNavigation extraction is intentionally NOT done: the ~16 select*/switchMode handlers are integration glue coupling nav state, the model hook, the viewport hook, and the editor guard — extracting them would create a ~25-parameter leaky abstraction with no behavior benefit. No further work warranted.

deferred.title: Routing exploration performance (candidate-volume redesign)
deferred.summary: planDiagram infinite-loops (reproduced in Node, exit 124) when a dense flow is rendered in a large, loosely-fitting view — e.g. hub-fan-dense (18 steps, 8 reciprocal pairs) in the corpus view-1 (Agent Turn Flow). The fitness gate never catches it because computeCorpusMetrics plans each flow in its TIGHT view (renderedViewFor); the live viewer lands flows[0] in its first compatible authored view, which can be large. A hang is worse than a bad route. Add a hard bound / budget so routing always terminates. AUDIT (2026-06-08/10): NOT an infinite loop — terminates ~17-24s on the densest flow (hub-fan-dense in view-1); the fitness gate already plans it. Whole-corpus profile shows it is candidate-VOLUME bound (sidePairs x ports x corridors; corridors scale with view size), with NO single hot spot (routeQualityFromSamples 5.3%, collisionCount 2.9%, corridorCandidate 2.8%, compareByRoutePriority 1.5%, GC 16%). A winner-preserving branch-and-bound gave no speedup (cost is evenly spread). The only real lever is a candidate-exploration REDESIGN (guided search / A* with pruning) — output-changing, requires full re-baseline + live-UI review of all 16 corpus diagrams. Redesign-scale, deliberately separate from the diagram-config PR.
deferred.rationale: Deferred because the 2026-06-10 optimization audit established this is candidate-volume bound (~722K candidates) with no isolated hot spot and no safe quick win — a routing-exploration redesign, not a 1.6.1-sized change. A branch-and-bound attempt was implemented and reverted after it produced no speedup and regressed the fan-parallel-bundle baseline. Needs its own design cycle. UPDATE (2026-06-11, user-reported on the flagship 18-step flow): in-browser measurement of the published 1.6.1 worker shows the audit conclusion incomplete for the browser path — candidate exploration is only ~15s of a ~120s plan; separateCloseParallelRoutes alone consumes ~100s. The work is therefore a targeted optimization of one named pass, not a full exploration redesign. Interim mitigation shipped: live planning progress (phase labels incl. the separation pass, edge counts, routes-considered counter, elapsed clock) so long plans demonstrate progress instead of a static spinner.

outOfScope.title: Crowded-face surface-selection refinements
outOfScope.summary: Two single-flow surface-selection niceties on faces carrying 5-6 edges, carried into 1.6.1 from 1.6.0. They were not taken inside the 1.6.0 cut because the diagrams were already clean (legible, zero crossings, zero sub-floor merges) and the fixes need distribution-pass surgery (re-spreading a crowded face so a moved pair lands >= the legibility floor from its neighbours) that carries regression risk to the 13 clean corpus flows. (1) skill-plugin-lifecycle: unify the use-skill/return pair onto unified-pipeline.south (line 7 currently mounts east); blocked by 6 edges on skill-plugin-system.west. (2) mail-operations: the config<->mail and operator<->mail pairs cross near mail-service, and the 9px approach legs (legible under the 4px standard) could spread further.
outOfScope.rationale: Obsolete on two independent grounds. (1) Targets no longer exist: the routing corpus was regenerated into abstract topology flows; skill-plugin-lifecycle, mail-operations, unified-pipeline.south and mail-service are gone. (2) The phenomenon is gone: the corpus baseline records closeParallelRuns=0, sharedSegments=0, laneOrderViolations=0, repeatedCrossings=0 across all 16 flows — the crowded-face/sub-legibility class this item targeted was eliminated by the routing overhaul. The residual 13 crossings sit on the deliberately adversarial stress flows and are baseline-sanctioned. Attempting distribution-pass surgery now would chase a nonexistent problem at pure regression risk.
outOfScope.decisionSource: 2026-06-10 investigative pass (baseline matrix over 16 corpus flows)

workstreams.name: Repository Intelligence
workstreams.summary: Expose repository structure and blast-radius understanding inside Architext.

workstreams.name: Diagram Ergonomics
workstreams.summary: Add layout controls that change diagram spacing without changing semantic data.

workstreams.name: Architecture Editing
workstreams.summary: Let users persist clarifying notes directly on architecture elements.

workstreams.name: Routing Polish
workstreams.summary: Finish the crowded-face surface-selection niceties deferred from the 1.6.0 routing overhaul.

workstreams.name: Viewer Refactor
workstreams.summary: Structural, behavior-preserving refactors of the viewer, starting with decomposing the App god-component.
